verses.text: Some teach circumcision is needful to salvation; Paul and Barnabas are sent to Jerusalem. A grave question. The gospel of grace is at stake.
Some teach circumcision is needful to salvation; Paul and Barnabas are sent to Jerusalem. A grave question. The gospel of grace is at stake.
They declare the Gentiles' conversion; the Pharisees say they must keep the law. The dispute. Some would bind grace with the law.
They declare the Gentiles' conversion; the Pharisees say they must keep the law. The dispute. Some would bind grace with the law.
They declare the Gentiles' conversion; the Pharisees say they must keep the law. The dispute. Some would bind grace with the law.
Peter says God 'put no difference,' purifying their hearts 'by faith.' One way. Jew and Gentile are saved alike by faith.
Peter says God 'put no difference,' purifying their hearts 'by faith.' One way. Jew and Gentile are saved alike by faith.
Peter says God 'put no difference,' purifying their hearts 'by faith.' One way. Jew and Gentile are saved alike by faith.
Peter says God 'put no difference,' purifying their hearts 'by faith.' One way. Jew and Gentile are saved alike by faith.
Why tempt God with an unbearable yoke? 'we shall be saved... through the grace of the Lord Jesus.' Grace alone. Salvation is by grace, not law-keeping.
Why tempt God with an unbearable yoke? 'we shall be saved... through the grace of the Lord Jesus.' Grace alone. Salvation is by grace, not law-keeping.
Barnabas and Paul tell the miracles; James says God did 'visit the Gentiles, to take out... a people.' A people for God. The Gentiles are part of His plan.
Barnabas and Paul tell the miracles; James says God did 'visit the Gentiles, to take out... a people.' A people for God. The Gentiles are part of His plan.
Barnabas and Paul tell the miracles; James says God did 'visit the Gentiles, to take out... a people.' A people for God. The Gentiles are part of His plan.
This agrees with the prophets, 'I will... build again the tabernacle of David,' that the residue may seek the Lord. Foretold. The ingathering was promised of old.
This agrees with the prophets, 'I will... build again the tabernacle of David,' that the residue may seek the Lord. Foretold. The ingathering was promised of old.
This agrees with the prophets, 'I will... build again the tabernacle of David,' that the residue may seek the Lord. Foretold. The ingathering was promised of old.
This agrees with the prophets, 'I will... build again the tabernacle of David,' that the residue may seek the Lord. Foretold. The ingathering was promised of old.
James's sentence: trouble not the Gentiles, but ask them to abstain from idols, fornication, and blood. Wise counsel. Liberty with regard for others' conscience.
James's sentence: trouble not the Gentiles, but ask them to abstain from idols, fornication, and blood. Wise counsel. Liberty with regard for others' conscience.
James's sentence: trouble not the Gentiles, but ask them to abstain from idols, fornication, and blood. Wise counsel. Liberty with regard for others' conscience.
They send the decision by chosen men, Judas and Silas, with Paul and Barnabas. Settled together. The church acts in unity.
They send the decision by chosen men, Judas and Silas, with Paul and Barnabas. Settled together. The church acts in unity.
They send the decision by chosen men, Judas and Silas, with Paul and Barnabas. Settled together. The church acts in unity.
They send the decision by chosen men, Judas and Silas, with Paul and Barnabas. Settled together. The church acts in unity.
They send the decision by chosen men, Judas and Silas, with Paul and Barnabas. Settled together. The church acts in unity.
'It seemed good to the Holy Ghost, and to us,' to lay no greater burden. Spirit-led. The decision is owned as the Spirit's own.
'It seemed good to the Holy Ghost, and to us,' to lay no greater burden. Spirit-led. The decision is owned as the Spirit's own.
'It seemed good to the Holy Ghost, and to us,' to lay no greater burden. Spirit-led. The decision is owned as the Spirit's own.
The letter brings consolation; Judas and Silas exhort the brethren. Comforted. The verdict gladdens the Gentile believers.
The letter brings consolation; Judas and Silas exhort the brethren. Comforted. The verdict gladdens the Gentile believers.
The letter brings consolation; Judas and Silas exhort the brethren. Comforted. The verdict gladdens the Gentile believers.
Sent away in peace, Paul and Barnabas continue teaching in Antioch. Fruitful labour. They go on building up the church.
Sent away in peace, Paul and Barnabas continue teaching in Antioch. Fruitful labour. They go on building up the church.
Sent away in peace, Paul and Barnabas continue teaching in Antioch. Fruitful labour. They go on building up the church.
Paul and Barnabas contend over John Mark and part; Barnabas takes Mark to Cyprus. A sad division. Even good men may sharply disagree.
Paul and Barnabas contend over John Mark and part; Barnabas takes Mark to Cyprus. A sad division. Even good men may sharply disagree.
Paul and Barnabas contend over John Mark and part; Barnabas takes Mark to Cyprus. A sad division. Even good men may sharply disagree.
Paul and Barnabas contend over John Mark and part; Barnabas takes Mark to Cyprus. A sad division. Even good men may sharply disagree.
Paul takes Silas and goes confirming the churches. Work goes on. The dispute does not halt the gospel's advance.
Paul takes Silas and goes confirming the churches. Work goes on. The dispute does not halt the gospel's advance.
